intermediate_skills:
IMPROVED SKILL SET (Bleve Repo Bugfix / CI Container)

0) Keep the “repo bugfix in CI container” mindset
- Focus only on fixing repository code/tests.
- No system/package installs; assume CI will provide toolchain even if container PATH is odd.

1) One-action protocol (retain, but be more output-safe)
- One response = exactly one

IMPROVED SKILL SET (Bleve repo bugfix in CI container)

0) Always obey the “one action” protocol + completion rule
- Every assistant turn: exactly ONE

IMPROVED SKILL SET (Bleve Repo Bugfix in CI Container)

A) Always anchor actions in /testbed (avoid “wrong directory” edits)
- Prefix EVERY shell command with: `cd /testbed && ...`
- When creating/overwriting files, never rely on current working dir; either:
  - `cd /testbed && cat > path/to/file <<'EOF' ... EOF`
  - or use absolute `/testbed/path/to/file`.

B) Toolchain reality check (Go may/may not exist in the interactive container)
- Before running Go commands, probe once:
  - `cd /testbed && command -v go && go version`
  - `cd /testbed && command -v gofmt && gofmt -w <file>`
- If Go is missing, continue with static analysis + patch + tests anyway (CI will run them), but DO add/adjust unit tests based on repository patterns.

C) Repository-specific knowledge to leverage (Bleve structure/patterns)
- Date/time parsers live under: `analysis/datetime/timestamp/{seconds,milliseconds,microseconds,nanoseconds}/`
- When fixing one parser, immediately compare with siblings to confirm intended logic:
  - Use siblings as canonical reference for:
    - parse error checks (`err != nil`),
    - range checks (outside bounds should error),
    - return values (`time.Unix(...)`, parser Name, nil error).
- Prefer matching conventions used in adjacent packages (comment style, var names, bounds semantics, etc.).

D) Debugging strategy improvements (faster + safer)
- Treat “inverted condition” bugs as: check `if err == nil` / `!= nil` and inequality direction first.
- Verify invariants explicitly:
  - Parse failures must return ErrInvalidTimestampString.
  - Range checks must reject OUTSIDE bounds (usually `x < min || x > max`, not `x > min && x < max`).
- When unsure about inclusive vs exclusive bounds, inspect other timestamp parsers and existing tests before choosing `<` vs `<=`.

E) Test-running hygiene (avoid common go test pitfalls)
- Never mix package patterns and explicit filenames in `go test`:
  - Good: `go test ./...`
  - Good: `go test ./analysis/datetime/timestamp/seconds -run TestName -count=1`
  - Bad: `go test ./... some_test.go` (causes “named files must be .go files: ./...”)
- Keep output small: use targeted package tests first, then broaden to `./...`.

F) Add regression tests when fixing behavior (even if bug seems obvious)
- For a parser bug, add a table-driven test in the same package:
  - include invalid strings (“not_a_number”, “”, “1.2”, “+”, “--1”)
  - include valid numeric strings (“0”, “1609459200”, negative timestamps if supported)
  - include extreme/boundary cases near min/max bounds if feasible.
- Assert both:
  - returned error type/value (ErrInvalidTimestampString vs ErrInvalidTimestampRange),
  - and returned time value for valid cases.

G) Code editing patterns: minimal, reviewable diffs
- Prefer small diffs:
  - edit only the condition lines; avoid rewriting the whole file.
  - preserve license/header/comment style unless necessary.
- Use a patch-oriented edit tool/pattern (apply_patch / perl -pi / sed) rather than full-file overwrite, unless the file is tiny and you can guarantee no collateral changes.
- After edits, always inspect: `cd /testbed && git diff` (keep diff focused).

H) Formatting/import hygiene (when Go tooling exists)
- Run `gofmt -w` on touched files.
- Re-run the smallest reproducer test after gofmt to ensure no accidental changes.

I) “One action” protocol + finalization discipline
- Each assistant turn: exactly one bash block with exactly one shell command (compound with `&&` is fine).
- Final response must be ONLY: `echo COMPLETE_TASK_AND_SUBMIT_FINAL_OUTPUT` (no extra prose).

You are helping an AI coding agent consolidate learned skills for fixing bugs.

## New Skills/Insights from Recent Evaluations
### Evaluation 1
```
IMPROVED SKILL SET (Bleve Repo Bugfix / CI Container)

0) Keep the “repo bugfix in CI container” mindset
```

```
- When fixing one parser, immediately compare with siblings to confirm intended logic:
  - Use siblings as canonical reference for:

- Prefer matching conventions used in adjacent packages (comment style, var names, bounds semantics, etc.).

D) Debugging strategy improvements (faster + safer)
- Treat “inverted condition” bugs as: check `if err == nil` / `!= nil` and inequality direction first.
- Verify invariants explicitly:
  - Parse failures must return ErrInvalidTimestampString.
  - Range checks must reject OUTSIDE bounds (usually `x < min || x > max`, not `x > min && x < max`).
- When unsure about inclusive vs exclusive bounds, inspect other timestamp parsers and existing tests before choosing `<` vs `<=`.

E) Test-running hygiene (avoid common go test pitfalls)
- Never mix package patterns and explicit filenames in `go test`:
  - Good: `go test ./...`
  - Good: `go test ./analysis/datetime/timestamp/seconds -run TestName -count=1`
  - Bad: `go test ./... some_test.go` (causes “named files must be .go files: ./...”)

F) Add regression tests when fixing behavior (even if bug seems obvious)
```

## Your Task
Merge all the above skills into a SINGLE, coherent skill set that:
1. Eliminates redundancy
2. Resolves conflicts (keep the most reliable/general one)
3. Prioritizes (most important first)
4. Stays concise

Provide the merged skills within a SINGLE ``` block.
llm_response:
```text
MERGED SKILL SET (Repo Bugfix in CI Container — Bleve)

1) One-action protocol + completion rule (highest priority)
- Every assistant turn: EXACTLY ONE bash block containing EXACTLY ONE shell command (you may chain with `&&`).
- Final turn must be ONLY: `echo COMPLETE_TASK_AND_SUBMIT_FINAL_OUTPUT` (no extra text).

2) CI-container mindset (scope + constraints)
- Fix repository code/tests only; do not install system packages or change the environment.
- Assume CI has the correct toolchain even if the interactive container is missing tools or has odd PATH.

3) Always anchor work in /testbed
- Prefix EVERY shell command with `cd /testbed && ...`.
- When creating/overwriting files, never rely on cwd; use `cd /testbed && cat > path/to/file <<'EOF' ... EOF` or absolute `/testbed/...`.

4) Toolchain reality check (probe once, then adapt)
- Before running Go tooling, probe: `cd /testbed && command -v go && go version` (and similarly for `gofmt` if needed).
- If Go tools are missing, still implement the fix + add/adjust tests based on repo patterns; CI will execute them.

5) Bleve-specific leverage: use sibling timestamp parsers as canonical reference
- Timestamp parsers live under `analysis/datetime/timestamp/{seconds,milliseconds,microseconds,nanoseconds}/`.
- When changing one parser, compare with siblings to match conventions: error handling (`err != nil`), range checks, return values (`time.Unix(...)`), naming, and semantics.

6) Debugging strategy: validate invariants first
- Suspect inverted conditions early (`err == nil` vs `!= nil`, inequality direction).
- Enforce invariants:
  - parse failures => `ErrInvalidTimestampString`
  - out-of-range => `ErrInvalidTimestampRange` using `x < min || x > max` style checks
- If inclusive/exclusive bounds are unclear, decide by inspecting sibling implementations and existing tests.

7) Regression tests are mandatory for behavior fixes
- Add table-driven tests in the same package for:
  - invalid strings: "", "not_a_number", "1.2", "+", "--1", etc.
  - valid numeric strings: "0", typical timestamps, negatives if supported
  - boundary/extreme cases near min/max where feasible
- Assert BOTH: correct error value/type and correct time value for valid inputs.

8) Test-running hygiene (avoid go test pitfalls; keep output small)
- Do not mix package patterns with explicit filenames in `go test`.
- Prefer targeted tests first, then broaden to `go test ./...`.

9) Minimal, reviewable diffs + formatting hygiene
- Make the smallest change that fixes the bug; preserve file style/headers.
- Prefer patch-style edits; avoid full-file rewrites unless trivial and safe.
- After edits: `cd /testbed && git diff` to confirm focus.
- If Go tooling exists, run `gofmt -w` on touched files and re-run the smallest relevant test.
```
final_skills:
MERGED SKILL SET (Repo Bugfix in CI Container — Bleve)

1) One-action protocol + completion rule (highest priority)
- Every assistant turn: EXACTLY ONE bash block containing EXACTLY ONE shell command (you may chain with `&&`).
- Final turn must be ONLY: `echo COMPLETE_TASK_AND_SUBMIT_FINAL_OUTPUT` (no extra text).

2) CI-container mindset (scope + constraints)
- Fix repository code/tests only; do not install system packages or change the environment.
- Assume CI has the correct toolchain even if the interactive container is missing tools or has odd PATH.

3) Always anchor work in /testbed
- Prefix EVERY shell command with `cd /testbed && ...`.
- When creating/overwriting files, never rely on cwd; use `cd /testbed && cat > path/to/file <<'EOF' ... EOF` or absolute `/testbed/...`.

4) Toolchain reality check (probe once, then adapt)
- Before running Go tooling, probe: `cd /testbed && command -v go && go version` (and similarly for `gofmt` if needed).
- If Go tools are missing, still implement the fix + add/adjust tests based on repo patterns; CI will execute them.

5) Bleve-specific leverage: use sibling timestamp parsers as canonical reference
- Timestamp parsers live under `analysis/datetime/timestamp/{seconds,milliseconds,microseconds,nanoseconds}/`.
- When changing one parser, compare with siblings to match conventions: error handling (`err != nil`), range checks, return values (`time.Unix(...)`), naming, and semantics.

6) Debugging strategy: validate invariants first
- Suspect inverted conditions early (`err == nil` vs `!= nil`, inequality direction).
- Enforce invariants:
  - parse failures => `ErrInvalidTimestampString`
  - out-of-range => `ErrInvalidTimestampRange` using `x < min || x > max` style checks
- If inclusive/exclusive bounds are unclear, decide by inspecting sibling implementations and existing tests.

7) Regression tests are mandatory for behavior fixes
- Add table-driven tests in the same package for:
  - invalid strings: "", "not_a_number", "1.2", "+", "--1", etc.
  - valid numeric strings: "0", typical timestamps, negatives if supported
  - boundary/extreme cases near min/max where feasible
- Assert BOTH: correct error value/type and correct time value for valid inputs.

8) Test-running hygiene (avoid go test pitfalls; keep output small)
- Do not mix package patterns with explicit filenames in `go test`.
- Prefer targeted tests first, then broaden to `go test ./...`.

9) Minimal, reviewable diffs + formatting hygiene
- Make the smallest change that fixes the bug; preserve file style/headers.
- Prefer patch-style edits; avoid full-file rewrites unless trivial and safe.
- After edits: `cd /testbed && git diff` to confirm focus.
- If Go tooling exists, run `gofmt -w` on touched files and re-run the smallest relevant test.
